entries.title: Recon chain (subfinder -> httpx -> nuclei -> notify)
entries.body:
A passive/active recon chain in one command: **subfinder -> httpx -> nuclei -> notify**. Subdomains, a live-host filter, a run of nuclei templates, a notification to a messenger. Artifacts are stored in a folder per domain. The tools are from ProjectDiscovery (MIT); reconftw (MIT) is the pipeline idea.

> It does not run what is not there: each step is checked with `command -v`. The run is active (httpx/nuclei send requests) - only against your own/authorized targets. notify.sh (see the neighboring script) sends the final alert.

deps: subfinder, httpx, nuclei (ProjectDiscovery). placeholders: `{{DOMAIN}}`, `{{WEBHOOK}}` (for notify).

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# recon_chain.sh - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
set -u
DOMAIN="${1:?usage: recon_chain.sh <domain> [webhook]}"
HOOK="${2:-}"
OUT="recon_$DOMAIN"; mkdir -p "$OUT"
need() { command -v "$1" >/dev/null || { echo "[!] missing: $1"; return 1; }; }

echo "[*] target=$DOMAIN out=$OUT/"

if need subfinder; then
  subfinder -d "$DOMAIN" -silent > "$OUT/subs.txt"
  echo "[+] subdomains: $(wc -l < "$OUT/subs.txt")"
fi

if need httpx; then
  httpx -silent -l "$OUT/subs.txt" -title -tech-detect -status-code \
        -o "$OUT/live.txt"
  # only URLs for nuclei
  httpx -silent -l "$OUT/subs.txt" > "$OUT/live_urls.txt"
  echo "[+] live hosts: $(wc -l < "$OUT/live_urls.txt")"
fi

if need nuclei; then
  nuclei -silent -l "$OUT/live_urls.txt" -severity low,medium,high,critical \
         -o "$OUT/nuclei.txt"
  FOUND=$(wc -l < "$OUT/nuclei.txt" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  echo "[+] nuclei findings: $FOUND"
fi

# notification (if there is a webhook and notify.sh nearby)
if [ -n "$HOOK" ] && [ -x ./notify.sh ]; then
  MSG="recon $DOMAIN: $(wc -l < "$OUT/live_urls.txt") live, $(wc -l < "$OUT/nuclei.txt" 2>/dev/null || echo 0) findings"
  ./notify.sh "$HOOK" "$MSG"
fi
echo "[=] done -> $OUT/"
```

Run:
```bash
bash recon_chain.sh target.com "https://hooks.slack.com/services/XXX"
```

entries.title: Webhook notifier (Slack / Telegram / Discord)
entries.body:
A universal **webhook notifier**: one script sends a message to Slack, Telegram or Discord - the type is determined from the URL. Called from any pipeline (recon_chain, monitoring) for alerts.

> The channel type is detected from the webhook domain: hooks.slack.com -> Slack JSON, api.telegram.org -> Telegram, discord.com/api/webhooks -> Discord. For Telegram, pass a URL of the form `https://api.telegram.org/bot<TOKEN>/sendMessage?chat_id=<ID>`.

deps: `curl`. placeholders: `{{WEBHOOK}}`, `{{MESSAGE}}`.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# notify.sh - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Sends to Slack/Telegram/Discord.
set -u
HOOK="${1:?usage: notify.sh <webhook-url> <message>}"
MSG="${2:?need message}"
# escape quotes for JSON
ESC=$(printf '%s' "$MSG" | sed 's/\\/\\\\/g; s/"/\\"/g')

case "$HOOK" in
  *hooks.slack.com*)
    curl -s -X POST "$HOOK" -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
      -d "{\"text\":\"$ESC\"}" >/dev/null && echo "[+] slack sent" ;;
  *api.telegram.org*)
    # HOOK already contains bot<token>/sendMessage?chat_id=...
    curl -s "$HOOK" --data-urlencode "text=$MSG" >/dev/null && echo "[+] telegram sent" ;;
  *discord.com/api/webhooks*|*discordapp.com/api/webhooks*)
    curl -s -X POST "$HOOK" -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
      -d "{\"content\":\"$ESC\"}" >/dev/null && echo "[+] discord sent" ;;
  *)
    # generic: POST as text
    curl -s -X POST "$HOOK" -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
      -d "{\"text\":\"$ESC\"}" >/dev/null && echo "[+] generic POST sent" ;;
esac
```

Run:
```bash
bash notify.sh "https://hooks.slack.com/services/XXX" "new finding on target.com"
bash notify.sh "https://api.telegram.org/bot123:ABC/sendMessage?chat_id=42" "ping"
```

entries.title: JS endpoint snapshot + diff + alert
entries.body:
Monitoring of **new endpoints in JS**: it fetches the target's JS files, pulls out paths/URLs with regexes, saves a snapshot and on the next run shows a **diff** - new endpoints often = a new feature = fresh attack surface. When a new one appears, it sends an alert via notify.sh.

> The LinkFinder/Endpoints-Monitor idea: a new path shows up in JS earlier than the feature in the UI. Run it on cron and you will see `/api/v2/internal/...` on deploy day.

deps: `curl`, `grep`, optional notify.sh. placeholders: `{{JS_URL}}` (or a file with a list of JS URLs), `{{WEBHOOK}}`.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# js_endpoint_diff.sh - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
set -u
SRC="${1:?usage: js_endpoint_diff.sh <js-url|file-of-urls> [webhook]}"
HOOK="${2:-}"
STORE=".js_endpoints"; mkdir -p "$STORE"

# build the list of JS URLs
if [ -f "$SRC" ]; then URLS=$(cat "$SRC"); else URLS="$SRC"; fi
KEY=$(printf '%s' "$URLS" | md5sum 2>/dev/null | cut -c1-10 || echo snap)
SNAP="$STORE/$KEY.txt"; NEW="$STORE/$KEY.new"

# extract paths and absolute URLs from the JS bodies
: > "$NEW"
for u in $URLS; do
  curl -s -k --max-time 15 "$u" \
  | grep -oaE '("|'"'"')(/[a-zA-Z0-9_./?=&%-]{2,}|https?://[a-zA-Z0-9_./?=&%-]+)("|'"'"')' \
  | tr -d "\"'" \
  | grep -aE '^(/|https?://)' >> "$NEW"
done
sort -u "$NEW" -o "$NEW"

if [ -f "$SNAP" ]; then
  ADDED=$(comm -13 "$SNAP" "$NEW")
  if [ -n "$ADDED" ]; then
    echo "[!] NEW endpoints:"; echo "$ADDED" | sed 's/^/    /'
    if [ -n "$HOOK" ] && [ -x ./notify.sh ]; then
      ./notify.sh "$HOOK" "new JS endpoints: $(echo "$ADDED" | head -5 | tr '\n' ' ')"
    fi
  else
    echo "[i] no new endpoints"
  fi
else
  echo "[*] baseline saved: $(wc -l < "$NEW") endpoints"
fi
cp "$NEW" "$SNAP"
echo "[=] snapshot -> $SNAP"
```

Run:
```bash
bash js_endpoint_diff.sh https://target/app.js "https://hooks.slack.com/services/XXX"
bash js_endpoint_diff.sh js_urls.txt
```

entries.title: curl -> Python requests converter
HAR -> Python requests series
entries.body:
A converter from a **HAR file to a series of Python `requests`**: you export the HAR from the browser (Network -> Save all as HAR), and the script turns each request into reproducible code. Handy for replaying a chain of requests, hunting IDOR/race across the series.

deps: stdlib. placeholders: `{{HAR}}` (the path to .har), `{{FILTER}}` (optional URL substring for filtering).

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# har_to_requests.py - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0).
import argparse, json, sys

SKIP_EXT = (".png", ".jpg", ".gif", ".css", ".woff", ".woff2", ".svg", ".ico")

def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="ARS3NAL HAR -> requests")
    ap.add_argument("har", help="path to .har file")
    ap.add_argument("-f", "--filter", default="", help="only URLs containing this")
    ap.add_argument("--keep-static", action="store_true")
    a = ap.parse_args()
    har = json.load(open(a.har, encoding="utf-8"))
    entries = har["log"]["entries"]
    print("import requests\n\ns = requests.Session()\n")
    n = 0
    for e in entries:
        req = e["request"]
        url = req["url"]
        if a.filter and a.filter not in url:
            continue
        if not a.keep_static and url.lower().split("?")[0].endswith(SKIP_EXT):
            continue
        method = req["method"]
        headers = {h["name"]: h["value"] for h in req.get("headers", [])
                   if not h["name"].startswith(":")}
        # cookies are already in the Cookie header; remove the noisy ones
        for noisy in ("Content-Length", "Host"):
            headers.pop(noisy, None)
        body = None
        pd = req.get("postData")
        if pd and "text" in pd:
            body = pd["text"]
        n += 1
        print(f"# [{n}] {method} {url}")
        print(f"r{n} = s.request({method!r}, {url!r},")
        print(f"    headers={json.dumps(headers)},")
        if body is not None:
            print(f"    data={body!r},")
        print(f"    verify=False)")
        print(f"print({n}, r{n}.status_code, len(r{n}.content))\n")
    if n == 0:
        sys.exit("[!] no matching requests (try without --filter or --keep-static)")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Run:
```bash
python3 har_to_requests.py session.har -f "/api/" > replay.py
python3 replay.py
```

entries.title: Baseline vs payload differ (status/len/time/similarity)
entries.body:
A core for blind detection: **it compares a baseline response with the response to a payload** by status, length, time and **text similarity** (difflib). It returns the deltas - so you can automatically decide "the response changed" in boolean/time-based tasks, without guessing the threshold by hand.

> It takes N baseline requests (the median time, a reference body), then the payload request. It prints: status change, length delta, time delta, similarity ratio (1.0 = identical). Similarity < ~0.98 at the same length is a common sign of a blind difference.

deps: `pip install requests`. placeholders: `{{URL}}` (with `*` at the payload point), `{{PAYLOAD}}`.

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# baseline_diff.py - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
import argparse, statistics, time, difflib, requests

def fetch(url, payload, timeout):
    full = url.replace("*", payload) if "*" in url else url
    t0 = time.time()
    r = requests.get(full, timeout=timeout, verify=False, allow_redirects=False)
    return r, time.time() - t0

def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="ARS3NAL baseline vs payload differ")
    ap.add_argument("-u", "--url", required=True, help="URL with * at injection point")
    ap.add_argument("-p", "--payload", required=True, help="payload to substitute at *")
    ap.add_argument("--baseline", default="", help="benign value for * (default: empty)")
    ap.add_argument("-n", "--samples", type=int, default=3)
    ap.add_argument("--timeout", type=int, default=30)
    a = ap.parse_args()
    requests.packages.urllib3.disable_warnings()

    # baseline: N runs, reference = first response, median time
    times, ref = [], None
    base_status = base_len = 0
    for _ in range(a.samples):
        r, dt = fetch(a.url, a.baseline, a.timeout)
        times.append(dt)
        if ref is None:
            ref, base_status, base_len = r.text, r.status_code, len(r.content)
    base_time = statistics.median(times)
    print(f"[*] baseline: status={base_status} len={base_len} time={base_time:.2f}s\n")

    # payload
    pr, pdt = fetch(a.url, a.payload, a.timeout)
    sim = difflib.SequenceMatcher(None, ref, pr.text).ratio()
    d_status = "SAME" if pr.status_code == base_status else f"{base_status}->{pr.status_code}"
    d_len = len(pr.content) - base_len
    d_time = pdt - base_time
    print(f"[=] payload {a.payload!r}")
    print(f"    status : {d_status}")
    print(f"    length : {d_len:+d} bytes")
    print(f"    time   : {d_time:+.2f}s")
    print(f"    similar: {sim:.4f}  (1.0 = identical)")
    changed = (pr.status_code != base_status) or abs(d_len) > 0 or sim < 0.98 or d_time > 1.0
    print(f"\n[=] {'DIFFERENT (signal)' if changed else 'no meaningful difference'}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Run:
```bash
python3 baseline_diff.py -u "https://t/item?id=1*" -p "1 AND 1=2" --baseline "1"
```
